title: Nested correlated subquery fails when indices used
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE users (id UInt32, name String, age Nullable(UInt32), created_at DateTime DEFAULT now());
CREATE TABLE comments (id UInt32, user_id UInt32, content String, created_at DateTime DEFAULT now(), INDEX idx_user_id (user_id));
INSERT INTO users (name, age) VALUES ('Alice', 30), ('Bob', NULL), ('Charlie', 25);
INSERT INTO comments (user_id, content) VALUES (1, 'First comment'), (2, 'Second comment'), (1, 'Third comment');
SELECT u.name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM comments c WHERE c.user_id = u.id) AS comment_count FROM users u WHERE u.age > (SELECT AVG(age) FROM users WHERE age IS NOT NULL) ORDER BY comment_count DESC;
key_bug_features: Nested correlated subquery with indices fails
